summary: Obfuscated postinstall drops and persists a platform-native binary
details: package.json declares `"postinstall": "node utils.cjs"`. utils.cjs is a 261 KB obfuscator.io-style script (RC4+base64 string-array decoder, self-defending console rebinders, setInterval debugger trap) whose decoded logic selects a per-OS payload from a DOWNLOAD_MAP (win32-x64.exe, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, linux-x64), fetches `${BINARY_BASE_URL}/${file}` with a hardcoded `Authorization: Bearer ${HF_TOKEN}` header, writes the binary as a hidden file under the user's local-data directory (LOCALAPPDATA / ~/Library/Application Support / XDG_DATA_HOME), chmods it 0o755 on POSIX, spawns it detached, and registers OS-level autostart (HKCU\...\Run on Windows, LaunchAgent on macOS, systemd unit on Linux). On non-Windows hosts the script re-spawns itself as a detached `node <script> --child` with stdio ignored and then calls `process.exit(0)`, so `npm install` returns success while the dropper continues in the background — an explicit anti-analysis pattern designed to hide the install-time work from CI logs and lifecycle observers. The package self-describes as a terminal logger; nothing about that purpose justifies a heavily obfuscated postinstall that downloads and persists an opaque platform-native executable. Multiple independent block signals stack: heavy obfuscation in lifecycle code, mutable remote binary fetch with bearer-token auth, purpose mismatch, multi-OS persistence install, and detach-and-exit anti-observation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
